metadata.title:
BROKEN WHEEL "


is The CAUSE

FOURTH LOCAL WRECK In A WEEK

Lumber Train Gets All Tangled Up
MEMS Legs and Arms Torn


Off--Pathetic Scenes.

pages.raw_text:
Four tramps probably dead and eight injured in
freight wreck the Northern Pacific this morning
half mile west of the Hangman creek bridge. Incoming
freight No. 602 thrown into the ditch by broken
wheel on the third car from the engine.


No tranmen hurt. The unidentified body Of one
tramp youth of about 25 horribly mutilated has been
recovered and the and legs of probably three
victims all tramps far is known, be under
the wreckage of the sixth


The locomotive of the train and the two following
did not leave the rails but the remainder of the ra
over 20 cars loaded mostly with lumber piled up in
space of about 10 rods.


When the wheel broke the third car, according to
witnesses of the accident that turned sdewse after
running several yards the ties and the train piled up
on top of it.


The train crew. all unhur consisted of Conductor R. J.
Mcdonald George Howarth, the brakeman, and
other brakeman. Engineer R. j. Lewis and his fireman
also escaped injury.
